text.KJV: 1:1 The Elder vnto the welbeloued Gaius, whom I loue in the trueth:
text.DRV: 1:1 The Senior to Gaius the dearest, whom I love in truth.
text.NABRE: 1:1 The Presbyter to the beloved Gaius whom I love in truth.
text.NRSV: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.

text.KJV: 1:2 Beloued, I wish aboue all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, euen as thy soule prospereth.
text.DRV: 1:2 My dearest, concerning all things I make my prayer that thou proceed prosperously, and fare well, as thy soul doth prosperously.
text.NABRE: 1:2 Beloved, I hope you are prospering in every respect and are in good health, just as your soul is prospering.
text.NRSV: Beloved, I pray that all may go well with you and that you may be in good health, just as it is well with your soul.

text.KJV: 1:3 For I reioyced greatly when the brethren came and testified of the truth that is in thee, euen as thou walkest in the trueth.
text.DRV: 1:3 I was exceeding glad when the brethren came, and gave testimony to thy truth, even as thou walkest in truth.
text.NABRE: 1:3 I rejoiced greatly when some of the brothers came and testified to how truly you walk in the truth.
text.NRSV: I was recently overjoyed when some of the friends arrived and testified to your faithfulness to the truth, namely how you walk in the truth.

text.KJV: 1:4 I haue no greater ioy, then to heare that my children walke in truth.
text.DRV: 1:4 Greater thank have I not of them, than that I may hear my children do walk in truth.
text.NABRE: 1:4 Nothing gives me greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
text.NRSV: I have no greater joy than this, to hear that my children are walking in the truth.

text.KJV: 1:5 Beloued, thou doest faithfully whatsoeuer thou doest to the Brethren, and to strangers:
text.DRV: 1:5 My dearest, thou doest faithfully whatsoever thou workest on the brethren, & that upon strangers.
text.NABRE: 1:5 Beloved, you are faithful in all you do for the brothers, especially for strangers;
text.NRSV: Beloved, you do faithfully whatever you do for the friends, even though they are strangers to you;

text.KJV: 1:6 Which haue borne witnesse of thy charitie before the Church: whome if thou bring forward on their iourney after a godly sort, thou shalt doe well:
text.DRV: 1:6 They have rendered testimony to thy charity in the sight of the Church: whom, thou shalt do well, bringing on their way in manner worthy of God.
text.NABRE: 1:6 they have testified to your love before the church. Please help them in a way worthy of God to continue their journey.
text.NRSV: they have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on in a manner worthy of God;

text.KJV: 1:7 Because that for his Names sake they went foorth, taking nothing of the Gentiles.
text.DRV: 1:7 For, for his name did they depart, taking nothing of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: 1:7 For they have set out for the sake of the Name and are accepting nothing from the pagans.
text.NRSV: for they began their journey for the sake of Christ, accepting no support from non-believers.

text.KJV: 1:8 We therefore ought to receiue such, that we might be fellow helpers to the trueth.
text.DRV: 1:8 We therefore ought to receive such: that we may be coadjutors of the truth.
text.NABRE: 1:8 Therefore, we ought to support such persons, so that we may be co-workers in the truth.
text.NRSV: Therefore we ought to support such people, so that we may become co-workers with the truth.

text.KJV: 1:9 I wrote vnto the Church, but Diotrephes, who loueth to haue the preeminence among them, receiueth vs not.
text.DRV: 1:9 I had written perhaps to the Church: but he that loveth to bear primacy among them, Diotrepes, doth not receive us.
text.NABRE: 1:9 I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to dominate, does not acknowledge us.
text.NRSV: I have written something to the church; but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, does not acknowledge our authority.

text.KJV: 1:10 Wherefore if I come, I will remember his deeds which he doeth, prating against vs with malicious words: and not content therewith, neither doth hee himselfe receiue the brethren, and forbiddeth them that would, and casteth them out of the Church.
text.DRV: 1:10 For this cause, if I come, I will advertise his works which he doeth: with malicious words chatting against us. And as though these things suffice him not, neither himself doth receive the brethren, and them that do receive, he prohibiteth, and casteth out of the Church.
text.NABRE: 1:10 Therefore, if I come, I will draw attention to what he is doing, spreading evil nonsense about us. And not content with that, he will not receive the brothers, hindering those who wish to do so and expelling them from the church.
text.NRSV: So if I come, I will call attention to what he is doing in spreading false charges against us. And not content with those charges, he refuses to welcome the friends, and even prevents those who want to do so and expels them from the church.

text.KJV: 1:11 Beloued, follow not that which is euill, but that which is good. He that doth good, is of God: but hee that doth euill, hath not seene God.
text.DRV: 1:11 My dearest, do not imitate evil, but that which is good. He that doeth well, is of God: he that doeth ill, hath not seen God.
text.NABRE: 1:11 Beloved, do not imitate evil but imitate good. Whoever does what is good is of God; whoever does what is evil has never seen God.
text.NRSV: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil but imitate what is good. Whoever does good is from God; whoever does evil has not seen God.

text.KJV: 1:12 Demetrius hath good report of all men, and of the trueth it selfe: yea, and we also beare record, and ye know that our record is true.
text.DRV: 1:12 To Demetrius testimony is given of all, and of the truth it-self, yea and we give testimony: and thou knowest that our testimony is true.
text.NABRE: 1:12 Demetrius receives a good report from all, even from the truth itself. We give our testimonial as well, and you know our testimony is true.
text.NRSV: Everyone has testified favorably about Demetrius, and so has the truth itself. We also testify for him, and you know that our testimony is true.

text.KJV: 1:13 I had many things to write, but I will not with inke and pen write vnto thee.
text.DRV: 1:13 I had many things to write unto thee: but I would not by ink and pen write to thee.
text.NABRE: 1:13 I have much to write to you, but I do not wish to write with pen and ink.
text.NRSV: I have much to write to you, but I would rather not write with pen and ink;

text.KJV: 1:14 But I trust I shall shortly see thee, and wee shall speake face to face. Peace bee to thee. Our friends salute thee. Greet the friends by name.
text.DRV: 1:14 But I hope forth-with to see thee, and we will speak mouth to mouth. Peace be to thee. The friends salute thee. Salute the friends by name.
text.NABRE: 1:14 Instead, I hope to see you soon, when we can talk face to face.
text.NRSV: instead I hope to see you soon, and we will talk together face to face.

text.NABRE: 1:15 Peace be with you. The friends greet you; greet the friends there each by name.
text.NRSV: Peace to you. The friends send you their greetings. Greet the friends there, each by name.

1.1: The elder, unto Gaius the beloved, whom, I, love in truth.
1.2: Beloved! concerning all things, I pray thou mayest be prospering, and be in health, even as, thy soul, is prospering,
1.3: For I rejoiced exceedingly, at brethren coming and bearing witness unto thy truth,—even as, thou, in truth, art walking.
1.4: I have no, greater, favour than these things, that I should be hearing that, my own children, in the truth, are walking.
1.5: Beloved! a faithful thing, art thou doing, whatsoever thou shalt accomplish for them that are brethren, and withal strangers,—
1.6: Who have borne witness to thy love before the assembly: whom thou wilt do, nobly, to set forward worthily of God.
1.7: For, in behalf of The Name, have they gone forth, taking, nothing, from them of the nations.
1.8: We, therefore ought to be sustaining such as these, that we may become, fellowworkers, with the truth.
1.9: I wrote something unto the assembly; but, he who is fond of taking the first place among them—Diotrephes, doth not make us welcome.
1.10: For this cause, if I come, I will bring to remembrance his works which he is doing,—with wicked words, prating against us; and, not content with these, he neither, himself, maketh the brethren welcome, but, them who are minded [to do it], he forbiddeth, and, out of the assembly, doth cast [them] .
1.11: Beloved! be not thou imitating what is bad, but what is good. He that doeth good, is, of God: he that doeth what is bad, hath not seen God.
1.12: Unto Demetrius, hath witness been borne by all and by the truth itself; howbeit, we also, bear witness, and thou knowest that, our witness, is, true.
1.13: Many things, had I to write unto thee,—but I am unwilling, with ink and pen, to be writing;
1.14: I hope, however, straightway, to see thee, and, mouth to mouth, will we talk. Peace be unto thee. The friends salute thee. Salute the friends by name.

1.1: The ancient to the dearly beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.
1.2: Dearly beloved, concerning all things I make it my prayer that thou mayest proceed prosperously, and fare well as thy soul doth prosperously.
1.3: I was exceedingly glad when the brethren came and gave testimony to the truth in thee, even as thou walkest in the truth.
1.4: I have no greater grace than this, to hear that my children walk in truth.
1.5: Dearly beloved, thou dost faithfully whatever thou dost for the brethren, and that for strangers,
1.6: Who have given testimony to thy charity in the sight of the church: whom thou shalt do well to bring forward on their way in a manner worthy of God.
1.7: Because, for his name they went out, taking nothing of the Gentiles .
1.8: We therefore ought to receive such, that we may be fellow helpers of the truth.
1.9: I had written perhaps to the church: but Diotrephes, who loveth to have the pre-eminence among them, doth not receive us.
1.10: For this cause, if I come, I will advertise his works which he doth, with malicious words prating against us. And as if these things were not enough for him, neither doth he himself receive the brethren, and them that do receive them he forbiddeth, and casteth out of the church.
1.11: Dearly beloved, follow not that which is evil, but that which is good. He that doth good, is of God: he that doth evil, hath not seen God.
1.12: To Demetrius testimony is given by all, and by the truth itself, yea and we also give testimony: and thou knowest that our testimony is true.
1.13: I had many things to write unto thee: but I would not by ink and pen write to thee.
1.14: But I hope speedily to see thee, and we will speak mouth to mouth. Peace be to thee. Our friends salute thee. Salute the friends by name.
